error.signature: HTTP 403: You have exceeded a secondary rate limit. Please wait a few minutes before you try again.

verdict.summary: GitHub has undocumented 'secondary' rate limits separate from the 5000 req/hr primary limit. Triggered by concurrent requests, rapid content creation, or polling. Not visible in X-RateLimit headers.

dead_ends.action: Check X-RateLimit-Remaining header and stay under 5000/hr
dead_ends.why_fails: Secondary rate limits are separate and invisible in standard rate limit headers. You can hit them at 100 req/hr if requests are concurrent.

dead_ends.action: Use a personal access token instead of GitHub App token
dead_ends.why_fails: Secondary rate limits apply to all authentication methods equally. PATs are not exempt.
GitHub may escalate to longer bans if you retry too aggressively after secondary rate limit.

workarounds.how: import time; time.sleep(1)  # between each write API call

workarounds.action: Use conditional requests with ETags for polling endpoints
workarounds.how: headers = {'If-None-Match': cached_etag}  # 304 responses don't count against limits

workarounds.action: Implement exponential backoff starting at 60s on 403, with jitter
